# Vendored: mp4box.js

## `mp4box.min.js`

- **Source**: npm registry, package `mp4box`, version `1.5.0` (`dist/mp4box.all.js`)
- **Upstream project**: https://github.com/gpac/mp4box.js
- **License**: BSD-3-Clause (see upstream `LICENSE`)
- **SHA-256**: `32b53bb2644f9615306bfc3f3ab6a0564d6121a95575ccefc93c9de3e8867c11`

Deliberately pinned to `1.5.0`, not the latest `2.4.1`. As of `2.4.1`, mp4box.js's
published `dist/mp4box.all.js` is a code-split ESM entrypoint that imports several
sibling chunk files (`rolldown-runtime-*.mjs`, `styp-*.mjs`) generated by its
Rolldown-based build -- not a single self-contained file, and not suitable for
vendoring as one static asset per this app's zero-bundler convention (see
`README.md`'s "Shared Architecture" section). `1.5.0` is the last release whose
`dist/mp4box.all.js` is a genuine standalone ES module (no imports, plain
`export { createFile, ... }` at the end) and was verified to still expose the
same `createFile` API used by `video_probe_browser.mjs`.

Only the trailing `//# sourceMappingURL=mp4box.all.js.map` comment was stripped
(no corresponding `.map` file is vendored, to avoid a dev-tools 404). No other
modification was made to the file content.

## Updating

1. `npm pack mp4box@<version>` and inspect `dist/mp4box.all.js` -- confirm it
   is still a single file with no `import` statements before using it.
2. Copy it to `mp4box.min.js`, strip any trailing `sourceMappingURL` comment.
3. Update the version and SHA-256 above (`shasum -a 256 mp4box.min.js`).
